subject: Re: LSJ Electric distribution-Charles Miller
body:
To:  Arran 
From: Charles  Miller 
Re: LSJ Electric   
April 6,  2008 
Arran, 
At a meeting with  Mr. Epstein it was asked if there could be an intermediate 
fix to the  present deficiency in the electrical switching and power 
distribution  system short of the proposed new WAPA transformer and new power 
distribution  service as envisioned in the proposed Utility Building scope of  work. 
I would like to  work with you to study possibilities and make 
recommendations to Mr.  Epstein.  
Can we with the  present WAPA transformer replace the existing 600 Amp switch 
mounted   to it with a larger switch? 
Is the present  wiring from the existing WAPA transformer mounted 600 Amp 
switch sized to take  greater then 600 Amps or need it be replaced. 
Is the 600 Amp  switch located in the generator room part of the outgoing 
power from the  generators or is it part of the incoming WAPA service and if so 
can it be  replaced with a larger switch. 
Assuming; 
    *   The switch/switch's  can be replaced with larger Amp switch's,   
    *   The  existing incoming WAPA transformer allows for a higher Amp 
mounted  switch,  
    *   The  wiring transformer to switch, to distribution is sufficient-  
    *   Will  the existing distribution panels allow for distribution of the 
existing loads  plus the addition of the Office Structure now in  
construction? 
    *   The  proposed switch to the transformer for the Office building is 
rated at 3 phase  480 Volt 120 Amps
Please review and  call me to discuss feasibility and economics of a 
temporary  fix. 
Thank  you, 
Charles  Miller 
914-488-5203 


In a message dated 4/4/2008 3:04:56 P.M. Eastern Daylight Time,  
rkahn@nysgmail.com writes:

Arran,  
I  just read this email to JEE and he thought it was excellent.  He wants  
you to be involved with all the details of the new mechanical equipment that  we 
are looking to purchase. Doug / Charles please make sure Arran has  
information available to him so he can review and comment.  Please call  me with any 
questions. 
Have  a nice weekend. 
Thank  you. 
 
Rich  Kahn

body_html:
To: Arran

From: Charles Miller

Re: LSJ Electric

April 6, 2008

 

Arran,

 

At a meeting with Mr. Epstein it was asked if there could be an intermediate fix to the present deficiency in the electrical switching and power distribution system short of the proposed new WAPA transformer and new power distribution service as envisioned in the proposed Utility Building scope of work.

 

I would like to work with you to study possibilities and make recommendations to Mr. Epstein. 

 

Can we with the present WAPA transformer replace the existing 600 Amp switch mounted  to it with a larger switch?

 

Is the present wiring from the existing WAPA transformer mounted 600 Amp switch sized to take greater then 600 Amps or need it be replaced.

 

Is the 600 Amp switch located in the generator room part of the outgoing power from the generators or is it part of the incoming WAPA service and if so can it be replaced with a larger switch.

 

Assuming;

  • The switch/switch's can be replaced with larger Amp switch's,
  • The existing incoming WAPA transformer allows for a higher Amp mounted switch,
  • The wiring transformer to switch, to distribution is sufficient-

 

  • Will the existing distribution panels allow for distribution of the existing loads plus the addition of the Office Structure now in construction?
  • The proposed switch to the transformer for the Office building is rated at 3 phase 480 Volt 120 Amps

Please review and call me to discuss feasibility and economics of a temporary fix.

 

Thank you,

 

Charles Miller

914-488-5203

 

 
In a message dated 4/4/2008 3:04:56 P.M. Eastern Daylight Time, rkahn@nysgmail.com writes:

Arran, 

I just read this email to JEE and he thought it was excellent.  He wants you to be involved with all the details of the new mechanical equipment that we are looking to purchase. Doug / Charles please make sure Arran has information available to him so he can review and comment.  Please call me with any questions.

Have a nice weekend.

Thank you.

Rich Kahn
